title: RUSTPY-0016 — posix ArgIterable args (posix_spawn argv/setsigdef, setgroups) eager-collect any iterable (CPython requires a sequence) -> unbounded memory
one_line_repro: import os, itertools; os.posix_spawn('/bin/true', ('/bin/true' for _ in itertools.count()), os.environ)
notes: The ArgIterable<T> mechanism collects the iterable whole then validates: posix_spawn argv (ArgIterable<OsPath>, posix.rs:1403/1525) balloons to OOM abort BEFORE spawning; setgroups (ArgIterable<RawGid>, :1337), posix_spawn setsigdef/setsigmask (ArgIterable<i32>, :1408/1417) are the same class but fill slower (small ints -> hang/unbounded-consume). CPython requires a real tuple/list/sequence in O(1). Fix = require a concrete container. WARRANTS a follow-up sweep of ALL ArgIterable argument sites. Instance of the eager-collect class.
